soap:
SOAP lancedb/lancedb#3599

```
REVIEW · lancedb/lancedb#3599 — fix(python): average MRR reciprocal ranks over all rankings
Ar-maan05 (regular) · S (+40/-1, 2 files)

ONE-LINER
  MRRReranker.rerank_multivector divided each document's summed reciprocal rank by the
  number of ranking systems it happened to appear in, instead of the total number of
  ranking systems being fused. Fix changes the divisor from len(reciprocal_ranks)
  (appearance count) to num_systems = len(vector_results) (total systems), matching
  standard MRR-fusion semantics (absence = reciprocal rank 0) and making
  rerank_multivector consistent with rerank_hybrid's existing 0-for-missing treatment.

FINDINGS  (each tied to ground truth)
  • python/python/lancedb/rerankers/mrr.py:151-157 — mrr_score_map[result_id] only
    appends a reciprocal rank when a doc appears in a given ranking table; a doc missing
    from a system contributes nothing to the list (not a 0). Confirmed by reading the
    accumulation loop directly — this is the root cause the PR describes, not a
    speculative claim.
  • python/python/lancedb/rerankers/mrr.py:165,168 (was line ~167 pre-fix, single line
    `mean_rr = np.mean(reciprocal_ranks)`) — old code's divisor was
    len(reciprocal_ranks), i.e. the appearance count. New code:
    num_systems = len(vector_results); mean_rr = sum(reciprocal_ranks) / num_systems.
    len(vector_results) is exactly the count of ranking tables passed into the
    function — the correct denominator for "all rankings," independent of how many the
    doc appears in. Verified by reading main's version of the file
    (git show main:python/python/lancedb/rerankers/mrr.py) side by side with the fix.
  • Hand-verified the arithmetic in the new test (test_rerankers.py:353-382) against
    both formulas:
      - New formula: doc1 = sum([1.0])/3 = 0.333; doc2 =
        sum([0.5,1.0,1.0])/3 = 0.833. Matches the asserted pytest.approx values exactly.
      - Old (buggy) formula on the same fixture: doc1 = mean([1.0]) = 1.0; doc2 =
        mean([0.5,1.0,1.0]) = 0.833. Under the old formula doc1 > doc2, so
        `scores[2] > scores[1]` and `result["_rowid"][0] == 2` would both fail — i.e.
        the new test genuinely fails on main and is not a test of the old (broken)
        behavior. Computed by hand (no pyarrow/pip in exec env), arithmetic deterministic.
  • Consistency check against rerank_hybrid (same file, lines 63-113): that function
    already computes vector_rr = 0.0 / fts_rr = 0.0 for a missing system before summing
    — the fix brings rerank_multivector in line; not a new invention.
  • Regression check on pre-existing test_mrr_reranker (test_rerankers.py:320-344,
    unchanged): duplicate-input case only asserts len equality, invisible to the divisor
    change. No regression risk.
  • _deduplicate (base.py:224-236) collapses rows by _rowid via np.unique — orthogonal
    to score computation; score dict built before dedup, looked up after. Not a concern.
  • Docstring alignment (lines 18-24): "MRR calculates the average of reciprocal ranks
    across different search results" — fix makes code match the docstring.
  • Edge cases: num_systems = len(vector_results) always >= 1 (ValueError early for
    empty vector_results, line 128-129), so no division-by-zero introduced.

  covered by: test_mrr_multivector_rewards_consensus (new, test_rerankers.py:353-382) —
  asserts exact scores for both the minority-consensus and majority-consensus doc, the
  ordering relationship, and final ranking order. Strong, specific regression test.
  NOT covered: rerank_hybrid path untouched and already correct — no test needed.

ASSESSMENT
  risk: low — scoped, well-isolated numerical fix to a pure scoring computation. No IO,
  concurrency, schema/wire, or unsafe. Refined risk down from baseline "med" (generic
  core-library prior) given the content.
  urgency: low — changes ranking output for a niche multi-vector-fusion reranker; not a
  crash/security/data-loss path.
  escalation: none (forced=false, no rule_ids).
  could not read: none — both changed files fully shown (patch_truncated: false), plus
  full file context pulled from the PR branch. Could NOT execute pytest (no pyarrow/pip
  in exec sandbox); the "fails on main / passes here" claim is hand-computed against the
  actual accumulation code on both branches. CI is green (merge_state.ci: green) and
  does include this test file — worth an attending glance at the CI log before co-sign.

DRAFT COMMENTS
  praise: Nice catch, and the PR description is unusually good — it derives the correct
  formula, shows a worked A/B example, and points at the sibling rerank_hybrid that
  already gets this right. Made verification fast.

  praise: test_mrr_multivector_rewards_consensus asserts exact expected scores via
  pytest.approx, not just an ordering check — right level of specificity for a
  regression test on a formula bug.

  nitpick: mrr.py:159-164 — the added comment is good but sits after the accumulation
  loop and before the num_systems assignment, reading slightly oddly since it explains a
  decision two statements below it. Consider moving it directly above
  `num_systems = len(vector_results)`. Non-blocking, stylistic.

RECOMMENDATION: approve
  confidence: high — root cause matches the PR description exactly on reading the code,
  corrected formula is standard MRR-with-fusion semantics and now consistent with the
  sibling rerank_hybrid, arithmetic in the new test checks out by hand against both
  formulas (old formula provably fails the new test), change fully isolated to a pure
  scoring computation.
  what would make this wrong: if some downstream caller relied on the old
  (appearance-count-divided) scores as intentional behavior. Found no evidence — the
  docstring, PR intent, and rerank_hybrid all agree on missing-system-as-zero. Also
  would reconsider if CI's green run did not actually execute this test file — worth a
  fast attending glance before co-signing.
```
